message.content:
Hello! I'm an expert Haskell programmer ready to help you with your project. 

I can assist you with:
- Analyzing Haskell code and requirements
- Creating well-documented GitHub issues for development tasks
- Understanding your codebase structure
- Reviewing existing code

What would you like to work on today?
message.reasoning_content: The user said "hi". I should greet them back and ask how I can help them with their Haskell project.

llmResponse.responseText:
Hello! I'm an expert Haskell programmer ready to help you with your project. 

I can assist you with:
- Analyzing Haskell code and requirements
- Creating well-documented GitHub issues for development tasks
- Understanding your codebase structure
- Reviewing existing code

What would you like to work on today?
llmResponse.responseThinking: The user said "hi". I should greet them back and ask how I can help them with their Haskell project.

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell programmer specialized in analyzing user requirements and Haskell code.
You prepare well-documented issues upon user demand.
Each individual issue you create will be executed by a human of software agent independently from each other and in no specific order. Hence, you should avoid creating multiple issues unless you are sure items will not conflict.
Your strategy should be as follows:
- avoid chunks that will generate conflicts (i.e., individual issues should not ask to modify the same file)
- repeat important data types between different tasks for a same project when they are used in multiple files
You mention important tradeoffs.
You can ask user questions, when doing so always assume the answer could be 'i do not know'.

contents.description: Search for a pattern in files using 'git grep'
contents.name: bash_grep-files

contents.description: Check Haskell code by compiling or testing
contents.name: bash_check-code

properties.propertyDescription: Either 'compile' to compile the code or 'test' to run tests

function.description: Check Haskell code by compiling or testing
function.name: bash_check-code

action.description: Either 'compile' to compile the code or 'test' to run tests

contents.description: creates a github issue using the 'gh' CLI
contents.name: bash_create_github_issue

properties.propertyDescription: comma-separated list of issue dependencies (e.g., '#42,#43'). If empty, the issue will be labeled 'agents/to-be-taken'. If non-empty, it will be labeled 'agents/wait'.
The base branch for this issue (e.g., 'gh-123'). All PRs for this issue will be against this branch. Defaults to 'main'.
Set to 'true' if this is the final task that merges the feature branch into 'main'.
the detailed description/body of the issue

function.description: creates a github issue using the 'gh' CLI
function.name: bash_create_github_issue

body.description: the detailed description/body of the issue

branch_name.description: The base branch for this issue (e.g., 'gh-123'). All PRs for this issue will be against this branch. Defaults to 'main'.

dependencies.description: comma-separated list of issue dependencies (e.g., '#42,#43'). If empty, the issue will be labeled 'agents/to-be-taken'. If non-empty, it will be labeled 'agents/wait'.

is_final.description: Set to 'true' if this is the final task that merges the feature branch into 'main'.

git-ref.description: the commit ref (short name or long sha) to read

contents.description: Displays interactive dialogs to ask the user for input using zenity. Supports text entry, yes/no questions, list selection, and file selection dialogs.
contents.name: bash_ask_user

properties.propertyDescription: Type of dialog: 'entry' for text input, 'question' for yes/no, 'list' for selection, 'file' for file picker
The prompt or question text to display in the dialog
For list dialogs: pipe-separated options (e.g., 'Option A|Option B|Option C')
For list dialogs: allow multiple selections (true/false)

function.description: Displays interactive dialogs to ask the user for input using zenity. Supports text entry, yes/no questions, list selection, and file selection dialogs.

multiple.description: For list dialogs: allow multiple selections (true/false)

options.description: For list dialogs: pipe-separated options (e.g., 'Option A|Option B|Option C')

text.description: The prompt or question text to display in the dialog

type.description: Type of dialog: 'entry' for text input, 'question' for yes/no, 'list' for selection, 'file' for file picker

contents.description: Displays large markdown-formatted text using markdown-eye viewer. The text is passed via stdin and the tool blocks until the user closes the viewer. Note: images are not supported.
contents.name: bash_display_large_text
